laws.title: Colorado Minimum Wage — $15.16/hr (Effective January 1, 2026)
laws.summary: Colorado's state minimum wage increased to $15.16/hr on January 1, 2026, up from $14.81/hr in 2025. The increase is CPI-indexed under Colorado's COMPS Order (Colorado Overtime and Minimum Pay Standards). Denver's local minimum wage is higher at $18.81/hr for 2026. Exempt salary thresholds also increased — ensure salaried exempt employees meet the new threshold under COMPS.
compliance_actions: Update payroll to $15.16/hr state minimum (or $18.81/hr if employees work in Denver)
Confirm exempt salary thresholds are met under the updated COMPS Order
Post the updated Colorado COMPS poster in your workplace
Update any written offer letters or employment agreements referencing prior rates

laws.title: Colorado AI Act (HB 24-1468) — High-Risk AI in Employment (Effective February 2026)
laws.summary: Colorado's SB 24-205 (AI Act) takes effect February 1, 2026 and applies to developers and deployers of 'high-risk' AI systems — including those used in employment decisions (hiring, firing, compensation, promotion). Employers must use reasonable care to avoid algorithmic discrimination, perform impact assessments, and provide notice and opportunity to appeal adverse decisions made by AI.
compliance_actions: Identify all AI/algorithmic tools used in employment decisions (hiring, firing, promotion, pay)
Conduct impact assessments for any 'high-risk' AI system before February 2026
Implement notice procedures — employees must be informed when AI makes high-stakes decisions
Establish an appeals process for adverse AI-driven employment decisions
Work with counsel on compliance as CDLE guidance is expected
